steps.title: Substitute the exponential function into the constraint
steps.content:
Express $f(n-1) = ab^{n-1}$ and $f(n) = ab^n$. Substitute both into the given equation:
$$ab^{n-1} = ab^n + \frac{82}{100}ab^{n-1}$$

steps.title: Collect terms with f(n-1) and factor
steps.content:
Move the $\frac{82}{100}ab^{n-1}$ term to the left side:
$$ab^{n-1} - \frac{82}{100}ab^{n-1} = ab^n$$
Factor the left side:
$$ab^{n-1}\left(1 - \frac{82}{100}\right) = ab^n$$
$$ab^{n-1} \cdot \frac{18}{100} = ab^n$$

steps.title: Divide by ab^(n-1) to solve for b
steps.content:
Divide both sides by $ab^{n-1}$ (non-zero since $a, b > 0$):
$$\frac{18}{100} = b$$
Simplify the fraction:
$$b = \frac{18}{100} = \frac{9}{50} = 0.18$$
